members.name: _GetContentsMinimumSize
members.summary: Virtual method to be implemented by the user. Overrides the value returned by [method get_contents_minimum_size].

members.name: AddThemeColorOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeColorOverride(StringName name, Color color)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme [Color] with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_color_override]. See also [method get_theme_color] and [method Control.add_theme_color_override] for more details.

members.name: AddThemeConstantOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeConstantOverride(StringName name, int constant)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme constant with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_constant_override]. See also [method get_theme_constant].

members.name: AddThemeFontOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeFontOverride(StringName name, Font font)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme [Font] with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_font_override]. See also [method get_theme_font].

members.name: AddThemeFontSizeOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeFontSizeOverride(StringName name, int fontSize)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme font size with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_font_size_override]. See also [method get_theme_font_size].

members.name: AddThemeIconOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeIconOverride(StringName name, Texture2D texture)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme icon with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_icon_override]. See also [method get_theme_icon].

members.name: AddThemeStyleboxOverride
members.signature: public void AddThemeStyleboxOverride(StringName name, StyleBox stylebox)
members.summary: Creates a local override for a theme [StyleBox] with the specified [param name]. Local overrides always take precedence when fetching theme items for the control. An override can be removed with [method remove_theme_stylebox_override]. See also [method get_theme_stylebox] and [method Control.add_theme_stylebox_override] for more details.

members.name: BeginBulkThemeOverride
members.summary: Prevents [code]*_theme_*_override[/code] methods from emitting [constant NOTIFICATION_THEME_CHANGED] until [method end_bulk_theme_override] is called.

members.name: CanDraw
members.summary: Returns whether the window is being drawn to the screen.

members.name: ChildControlsChanged
members.summary: Requests an update of the [Window] size to fit underlying [Control] nodes.

members.name: EndBulkThemeOverride
members.summary: Ends a bulk theme override update. See [method begin_bulk_theme_override].

members.name: GetContentsMinimumSize
members.summary: Returns the combined minimum size from the child [Control] nodes of the window. Use [method child_controls_changed] to update it when child nodes have changed. The value returned by this method can be overridden with [method _get_contents_minimum_size].

members.name: GetFlag
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param flag] is set.

members.name: GetFocusedWindow
members.summary: Returns the focused window.

members.name: GetLayoutDirection
members.summary: Returns layout direction and text writing direction.

members.name: GetOutputMaxLinearValue
members.summary: Returns the maximum value for linear color components that can be displayed in this window, regardless of SDR or HDR output. Returns [code]1.0[/code] if HDR is not enabled or not supported. The [signal output_max_linear_value_changed] signal will be emitted whenever this value changes. This value is used by tonemapping and other [Environment] effects to ensure that bright colors are presented in the range that can be displayed by this window. When using this maximum linear value in your project, it should only be used to present colors directly to the screen without tonemapping and without influencing lighting, post-processing effects, or surrounding color. The following is an example that produces the brightest purple color that the screen can produce: [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _process(_delta): # output_max_linear_value may change often, so do this every frame. var max_linear_value = get_window().get_output_max_linear_value() # Replace this with your color: var original_color = Color.PURPLE # Normalize to max_linear_value to produce the brightest color possible, # regardless of SDR or HDR output: var bright_color = normalize_color(original_color, max_linear_value) func normalize_color(srgb_color, max_linear_value = 1.0): # Color must be linear-encoded to use math operations. var linear_color = srgb_color.srgb_to_linear() var max_rgb_value = maxf(linear_color.r, maxf(linear_color.g, linear_color.b)) var brightness_scale = max_linear_value / max_rgb_value linear_color *= brightness_scale # Undo changes to the alpha channel, which should not be modified. linear_color.a = srgb_color.a # Convert back to nonlinear sRGB encoding, which is required for Color in # Godot unless stated otherwise. return linear_color.linear_to_srgb() [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] You will need to convert sRGB colors to linear before multiplying by this value to get correct results.

members.name: GetPositionWithDecorations
members.summary: Returns the window's position including its border. [b]Note:[/b] If [member visible] is [code]false[/code], this method returns the same value as [member position].

members.name: GetSizeWithDecorations
members.summary: Returns the window's size including its border. [b]Note:[/b] If [member visible] is [code]false[/code], this method returns the same value as [member size].

members.name: GetThemeColor
members.signature: public Color GetThemeColor(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns a [Color] from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a color item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for more details.

members.name: GetThemeConstant
members.signature: public int GetThemeConstant(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns a constant from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a constant item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for more details.

members.name: GetThemeDefaultBaseScale
members.summary: Returns the default base scale value from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a valid [member Theme.default_base_scale] value. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeDefaultFont
members.summary: Returns the default font from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a valid [member Theme.default_font] value. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeDefaultFontSize
members.summary: Returns the default font size value from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a valid [member Theme.default_font_size] value. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeFont
members.signature: public Font GetThemeFont(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns a [Font] from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a font item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeFontSize
members.signature: public int GetThemeFontSize(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns a font size from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a font size item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeIcon
members.signature: public Texture2D GetThemeIcon(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns an icon from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has an icon item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetThemeStylebox
members.signature: public StyleBox GetThemeStylebox(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns a [StyleBox] from the first matching [Theme] in the tree if that [Theme] has a stylebox item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: GetWindowId
members.summary: Returns the ID of the window.

members.name: GrabFocus
members.summary: Causes the window to grab focus, allowing it to receive user input.

members.name: HasFocus
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the window is focused.

members.name: HasThemeColor
members.signature: public bool HasThemeColor(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has a color item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeColorOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme [Color] with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_color_override].

members.name: HasThemeConstant
members.signature: public bool HasThemeConstant(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has a constant item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeConstantOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme constant with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_constant_override].

members.name: HasThemeFont
members.signature: public bool HasThemeFont(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has a font item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeFontOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme [Font] with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_font_override].

members.name: HasThemeFontSize
members.signature: public bool HasThemeFontSize(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has a font size item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeFontSizeOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme font size with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_font_size_override].

members.name: HasThemeIcon
members.signature: public bool HasThemeIcon(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has an icon item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeIconOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme icon with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_icon_override].

members.name: HasThemeStylebox
members.signature: public bool HasThemeStylebox(StringName name, StringName themeType)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a matching [Theme] in the tree that has a stylebox item with the specified [param name] and [param theme_type]. See [method Control.get_theme_color] for details.

members.name: HasThemeStyleboxOverride
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is a local override for a theme [StyleBox] with the specified [param name] in this [Control] node. See [method add_theme_stylebox_override].

members.name: Hide
members.summary: Hides the window. This is not the same as minimized state. Hidden window can't be interacted with and needs to be made visible with [method show].

members.name: IsEmbedded
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the window is currently embedded in another window.

members.name: IsLayoutRtl
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the layout is right-to-left.

members.name: IsMaximizeAllowed
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the window can be maximized (the maximize button is enabled).

members.name: IsUsingFontOversampling
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if font oversampling is enabled. See [method set_use_font_oversampling].

members.name: MoveToCenter
members.summary: Centers the window in the current screen. If the window is embedded, it is centered in the embedder [Viewport] instead.

members.name: MoveToForeground
members.summary: Causes the window to grab focus, allowing it to receive user input.

members.name: Popup
members.summary: Shows the [Window] and makes it transient (see [member transient]). If [param rect] is provided, it will be set as the [Window]'s size. Fails if called on the main window. If [member ProjectSettings.display/window/subwindows/embed_subwindows] is [code]true[/code] (single-window mode), [param rect]'s coordinates are global and relative to the main window's top-left corner (excluding window decorations). If [param rect]'s position coordinates are negative, the window will be located outside the main window and may not be visible as a result. If [member ProjectSettings.display/window/subwindows/embed_subwindows] is [code]false[/code] (multi-window mode), [param rect]'s coordinates are global and relative to the top-left corner of the leftmost screen. If [param rect]'s position coordinates are negative, the window will be placed at the top-left corner of the screen. [b]Note:[/b] [param rect] must be in global coordinates if specified.

members.name: PopupCentered
members.summary: Popups the [Window] at the center of the current screen, with optionally given minimum size. If the [Window] is embedded, it will be centered in the parent [Viewport] instead. [b]Note:[/b] Calling it with the default value of [param minsize] is equivalent to calling it with [member size].

members.name: PopupCenteredClamped
members.signature: public void PopupCenteredClamped(Vector2i minsize, float fallbackRatio)
members.summary: Popups the [Window] centered inside its parent [Window]. [param fallback_ratio] determines the maximum size of the [Window], in relation to its parent. [b]Note:[/b] Calling it with the default value of [param minsize] is equivalent to calling it with [member size].

members.name: PopupCenteredRatio
members.summary: If [Window] is embedded, popups the [Window] centered inside its embedder and sets its size as a [param ratio] of embedder's size. If [Window] is a native window, popups the [Window] centered inside the screen of its parent [Window] and sets its size as a [param ratio] of the screen size.

members.name: PopupExclusive
members.signature: public void PopupExclusive(Node fromNode, Rect2i rect)
members.summary: Attempts to parent this dialog to the last exclusive window relative to [param from_node], and then calls [method Window.popup] on it. The dialog must have no current parent, otherwise the method fails. See also [method set_unparent_when_invisible] and [method Node.get_last_exclusive_window].

members.name: PopupExclusiveCentered
members.signature: public void PopupExclusiveCentered(Node fromNode, Vector2i minsize)
members.summary: Attempts to parent this dialog to the last exclusive window relative to [param from_node], and then calls [method Window.popup_centered] on it. The dialog must have no current parent, otherwise the method fails. See also [method set_unparent_when_invisible] and [method Node.get_last_exclusive_window].

members.name: PopupExclusiveCenteredClamped
members.signature: public void PopupExclusiveCenteredClamped(Node fromNode, Vector2i minsize, float fallbackRatio)
members.summary: Attempts to parent this dialog to the last exclusive window relative to [param from_node], and then calls [method Window.popup_centered_clamped] on it. The dialog must have no current parent, otherwise the method fails. See also [method set_unparent_when_invisible] and [method Node.get_last_exclusive_window].

members.name: PopupExclusiveCenteredRatio
members.signature: public void PopupExclusiveCenteredRatio(Node fromNode, float ratio)
members.summary: Attempts to parent this dialog to the last exclusive window relative to [param from_node], and then calls [method Window.popup_centered_ratio] on it. The dialog must have no current parent, otherwise the method fails. See also [method set_unparent_when_invisible] and [method Node.get_last_exclusive_window].

members.name: PopupExclusiveOnParent
members.signature: public void PopupExclusiveOnParent(Node fromNode, Rect2i parentRect)
members.summary: Attempts to parent this dialog to the last exclusive window relative to [param from_node], and then calls [method Window.popup_on_parent] on it. The dialog must have no current parent, otherwise the method fails. See also [method set_unparent_when_invisible] and [method Node.get_last_exclusive_window].

members.name: PopupOnParent
members.summary: Popups the [Window] with a position shifted by parent [Window]'s position. If the [Window] is embedded, has the same effect as [method popup].

members.name: RemoveThemeColorOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme [Color] with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_color_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RemoveThemeConstantOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme constant with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_constant_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RemoveThemeFontOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme [Font] with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_font_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RemoveThemeFontSizeOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme font size with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_font_size_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RemoveThemeIconOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme icon with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_icon_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RemoveThemeStyleboxOverride
members.summary: Removes a local override for a theme [StyleBox] with the specified [param name] previously added by [method add_theme_stylebox_override] or via the Inspector dock.

members.name: RequestAttention
members.summary: Tells the OS that the [Window] needs an attention. This makes the window stand out in some way depending on the system, e.g. it might blink on the task bar.

members.name: ResetSize
members.summary: Resets the size to the minimum size, which is the max of [member min_size] and (if [member wrap_controls] is enabled) [method get_contents_minimum_size]. This is equivalent to calling [code]set_size(Vector2i())[/code] (or any size below the minimum).

members.name: SetFlag
members.signature: public void SetFlag(int flag, bool enabled)
members.summary: Sets a specified window flag.

members.name: SetImeActive
members.summary: If [param active] is [code]true[/code], enables system's native IME (Input Method Editor).

members.name: SetImePosition
members.summary: Moves IME to the given position.

members.name: SetLayoutDirection
members.summary: Sets layout direction and text writing direction. Right-to-left layouts are necessary for certain languages (e.g. Arabic and Hebrew).

members.name: SetTaskbarProgressState
members.summary: Sets the type and state of the progress bar on the taskbar/dock icon of the [Window]. See [enum DisplayServer.ProgressState] for possible values and how each mode behaves. [b]Note:[/b] This method is implemented only on Windows and macOS.

members.name: SetTaskbarProgressValue
members.summary: Creates a progress bar on the taskbar/dock icon of the [Window] if it does not exist, sets the progress of the icon. [param value] acts as a relative percentage value, ranges from [code]0.0[/code] (lowest) to [code]1.0[/code] (highest). [b]Note:[/b] This method is implemented only on Windows and macOS.

members.name: SetUnparentWhenInvisible
members.summary: If [param unparent] is [code]true[/code], the window is automatically unparented when going invisible. [b]Note:[/b] Make sure to keep a reference to the node, otherwise it will be orphaned. You also need to manually call [method Node.queue_free] to free the window if it's not parented.

members.name: SetUseFontOversampling
members.summary: Enables font oversampling. This makes fonts look better when they are scaled up.

members.name: Show
members.summary: Makes the [Window] appear. This enables interactions with the [Window] and doesn't change any of its property other than visibility (unlike e.g. [method popup]).

members.name: StartDrag
members.summary: Starts an interactive drag operation on the window, using the current mouse position. Call this method when handling a mouse button being pressed to simulate a pressed event on the window's title bar. Using this method allows the window to participate in space switching, tiling, and other system features.

members.name: StartResize
members.summary: Starts an interactive resize operation on the window, using the current mouse position. Call this method when handling a mouse button being pressed to simulate a pressed event on the window's edge.

members.name: AccessibilityDescription
members.summary: The human-readable node description that is reported to assistive apps.

members.name: AccessibilityName
members.summary: The human-readable node name that is reported to assistive apps.

members.name: AlwaysOnTop
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the window will be on top of all other windows. Does not work if [member transient] is enabled.

members.name: AutoTranslate
members.summary: Toggles if any text should automatically change to its translated version depending on the current locale.

members.name: Borderless
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the window will have no borders.

members.name: ContentScaleAspect
members.summary: Specifies how the content's aspect behaves when the [Window] is resized. The base aspect is determined by [member content_scale_size].

members.name: ContentScaleFactor
members.summary: Specifies the base scale of [Window]'s content when its [member size] is equal to [member content_scale_size]. See also [method Viewport.get_stretch_transform].

members.name: ContentScaleMode
members.summary: Specifies how the content is scaled when the [Window] is resized.

members.name: ContentScaleSize
members.summary: The content's base size in "virtual" pixels. Not to be confused with [member size], which sets the actual window's physical size in pixels. If set to a value greater than [code]0[/code] and [member content_scale_mode] is set to a value other than [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_DISABLED], the [Window]'s content will be scaled when the window is resized to a different size. Higher values will make the content appear [i]smaller[/i], as it will be able to fit more of the project in view. On the root [Window], this is set to match [member ProjectSettings.display/window/size/viewport_width] and [member ProjectSettings.display/window/size/viewport_height] by default. For example, when using [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_CANVAS_ITEMS] and [member content_scale_size] set to [code]Vector2i(1280, 720)[/code], using a window size of [code]2560×1440[/code] will make 2D elements appear at double their original size, as the content is scaled by a factor of [code]2.0[/code] ([code]2560.0 / 1280.0 = 2.0[/code], [code]1440.0 / 720.0 = 2.0[/code]). See [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html#base-size]the Base size section of the Multiple resolutions documentation[/url] for details.

members.name: ContentScaleStretch
members.summary: The policy to use to determine the final scale factor for 2D elements. This affects how [member content_scale_factor] is applied, in addition to the automatic scale factor determined by [member content_scale_size].

members.name: CurrentScreen
members.summary: The screen the window is currently on.

members.name: ExcludeFromCapture
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] is excluded from screenshots taken by [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image], [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image_rect], and [method DisplayServer.screen_get_pixel]. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented on macOS and Windows. [b]Note:[/b] Enabling this setting will prevent standard screenshot methods from capturing a window image, but does [b]NOT[/b] guarantee that other apps won't be able to capture an image. It should not be used as a DRM or security measure.

members.name: Exclusive
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] will be in exclusive mode. Exclusive windows are always on top of their parent and will block all input going to the parent [Window]. Needs [member transient] enabled to work.

members.name: ExtendToTitle
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] contents is expanded to the full size of the window, window title bar is transparent. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented only on macOS. [b]Note:[/b] This property only works with native windows.

members.name: ForceNative
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], native window will be used regardless of parent viewport and project settings.

members.name: HdrOutputRequested
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], requests HDR output for the [Window], falling back to SDR if not supported, and automatically switching between HDR and SDR as the window moves between screens, screen capabilities change, or system settings are modified. This will internally force [member Viewport.use_hdr_2d] to be enabled on the main [Viewport]. All other [SubViewport] of this [Window] must have their [member Viewport.use_hdr_2d] property enabled to produce HDR output.

members.name: InitialPosition
members.summary: Specifies the initial type of position for the [Window].

members.name: KeepTitleVisible
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] width is expanded to keep the title bar text fully visible.

members.name: MaxSize
members.summary: If non-zero, the [Window] can't be resized to be bigger than this size. [b]Note:[/b] This property will be ignored if the value is lower than [member min_size].

members.name: MaximizeDisabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window]'s maximize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] If both minimize and maximize buttons are disabled, buttons are fully hidden, and only close button is visible. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented only on macOS and Windows.

members.name: MinSize
members.summary: If non-zero, the [Window] can't be resized to be smaller than this size. [b]Note:[/b] This property will be ignored in favor of [method get_contents_minimum_size] if [member wrap_controls] is enabled and if its size is bigger.

members.name: MinimizeDisabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window]'s minimize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] If both minimize and maximize buttons are disabled, buttons are fully hidden, and only close button is visible. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented only on macOS and Windows.

members.name: Mode
members.summary: Set's the window's current mode. [b]Note:[/b] Fullscreen mode is not exclusive full screen on Windows and Linux. [b]Note:[/b] This method only works with native windows, i.e. the main window and [Window]-derived nodes when [member Viewport.gui_embed_subwindows] is disabled in the main viewport.

members.name: MousePassthrough
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], all mouse events will be passed to the underlying window of the same application. See also [member mouse_passthrough_polygon]. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS and Windows. [b]Note:[/b] This property only works with native windows.

members.name: MousePassthroughPolygon
members.summary: Sets a polygonal region of the window which accepts mouse events. Mouse events outside the region will be passed through. Passing an empty array will disable passthrough support (all mouse events will be intercepted by the window, which is the default behavior). [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Set region, using Path2D node. $Window.mouse_passthrough_polygon = $Path2D.curve.get_baked_points() # Set region, using Polygon2D node. $Window.mouse_passthrough_polygon = $Polygon2D.polygon # Reset region to default. $Window.mouse_passthrough_polygon = [] [/gdscript] [csharp] // Set region, using Path2D node. GetNode<Window>("Window").MousePassthroughPolygon = GetNode<Path2D>("Path2D").Curve.GetBakedPoints(); // Set region, using Polygon2D node. GetNode<Window>("Window").MousePassthroughPolygon = GetNode<Polygon2D>("Polygon2D").Polygon; // Reset region to default. GetNode<Window>("Window").MousePassthroughPolygon = []; [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] This property is ignored if [member mouse_passthrough] is set to [code]true[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] On Windows, the portion of a window that lies outside the region is not drawn, while on Linux (X11) and macOS it is. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS and Windows.

members.name: NonclientArea
members.summary: If set, defines the window's custom decoration area which will receive mouse input, even if normal input to the window is blocked (such as when it has an exclusive child opened). See also [signal nonclient_window_input].

members.name: PopupWindow
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] will be considered a popup. Popups are sub-windows that don't show as separate windows in system's window manager's window list and will send close request when anything is clicked outside of them (unless [member exclusive] is enabled).

members.name: PopupWmHint
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] will signal to the window manager that it is supposed to be an implementation-defined "popup" (usually a floating, borderless, untileable and immovable child window).

members.name: Position
members.summary: The window's position in pixels. If [member ProjectSettings.display/window/subwindows/embed_subwindows] is [code]false[/code], the position is in absolute screen coordinates. This typically applies to editor plugins. If the setting is [code]true[/code], the window's position is in the coordinates of its parent [Viewport]. [b]Note:[/b] This property only works if [member initial_position] is set to [constant WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_ABSOLUTE].

members.name: SharpCorners
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] will override the OS window style to display sharp corners. [b]Note:[/b] This property is implemented only on Windows (11). [b]Note:[/b] This property only works with native windows.

members.name: Size
members.summary: The window's size in pixels. See also [member content_scale_size], which doesn't set the window's physical size but affects how scaling works relative to the current [member content_scale_mode].

members.name: Theme
members.summary: The [Theme] resource this node and all its [Control] and [Window] children use. If a child node has its own [Theme] resource set, theme items are merged with child's definitions having higher priority. [b]Note:[/b] [Window] styles will have no effect unless the window is embedded.

members.name: ThemeTypeVariation
members.summary: The name of a theme type variation used by this [Window] to look up its own theme items. See [member Control.theme_type_variation] for more details.

members.name: Title
members.summary: The window's title. If the [Window] is native, title styles set in [Theme] will have no effect.

members.name: Transient
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] is transient, i.e. it's considered a child of another [Window]. The transient window will be destroyed with its transient parent and will return focus to their parent when closed. The transient window is displayed on top of a non-exclusive full-screen parent window. Transient windows can't enter full-screen mode. Note that behavior might be different depending on the platform.

members.name: TransientToFocused
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], and the [Window] is [member transient], this window will (at the time of becoming visible) become transient to the currently focused window instead of the immediate parent window in the hierarchy. Note that the transient parent is assigned at the time this window becomes visible, so changing it afterwards has no effect until re-shown.

members.name: Transparent
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window]'s background can be transparent. This is best used with embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Linux, macOS and Windows, but availability might vary depending on GPU driver, display manager, and compositor capabilities. [b]Note:[/b] This property has no effect if [member ProjectSettings.display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed] is set to [code]false[/code].

members.name: Unfocusable
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Window] can't be focused nor interacted with. It can still be visible.

members.name: Unresizable
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the window can't be resized.

members.name: Visible
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the window is visible.

members.name: WrapControls
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the window's size will automatically update when a child node is added or removed, ignoring [member min_size] if the new size is bigger. If [code]false[/code], you need to call [method child_controls_changed] manually.

signals.name: AboutToPopup
signals.summary: Emitted right after [method popup] call, before the [Window] appears or does anything.

signals.name: CloseRequested
signals.summary: Emitted when the [Window]'s close button is pressed or when [member popup_window] is enabled and user clicks outside the window. This signal can be used to handle window closing, e.g. by connecting it to [method hide].

signals.name: DpiChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the [Window]'s DPI changes as a result of OS-level changes (e.g. moving the window from a Retina display to a lower resolution one). [b]Note:[/b] Only implemented on macOS and Linux (Wayland).

signals.name: FilesDropped
signals.summary: Emitted when files are dragged from the OS file manager and dropped in the game window. The argument is a list of file paths. [codeblock] func _ready(): get_window().files_dropped.connect(on_files_dropped) func on_files_dropped(files): print(files) [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] This signal only works with native windows, i.e. the main window and [Window]-derived nodes when [member Viewport.gui_embed_subwindows] is disabled in the main viewport.

signals.name: FocusExited
signals.summary: Emitted when the [Window] loses its focus.

signals.name: GoBackRequested
signals.summary: Emitted when a go back request is sent (e.g. pressing the "Back" button on Android), right after [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_WM_GO_BACK_REQUEST].

signals.name: MouseEntered
signals.summary: Emitted when the mouse cursor enters the [Window]'s visible area, that is not occluded behind other [Control]s or windows, provided its [member Viewport.gui_disable_input] is [code]false[/code] and regardless if it's currently focused or not.

signals.name: MouseExited
signals.summary: Emitted when the mouse cursor leaves the [Window]'s visible area, that is not occluded behind other [Control]s or windows, provided its [member Viewport.gui_disable_input] is [code]false[/code] and regardless if it's currently focused or not.

signals.name: NonclientWindowInput
signals.summary: Emitted when the mouse event is received by the custom decoration area defined by [member nonclient_area], and normal input to the window is blocked (such as when it has an exclusive child opened). [param event]'s position is in the embedder's coordinate system.

signals.name: OutputMaxLinearValueChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the output max linear value returned by [method Window.get_output_max_linear_value] has changed. This occurs when HDR output is enabled or disabled and when any HDR output luminance values of the window have changed, such as when the player adjusts their screen brightness setting or moves the window to a different screen. [param output_max_linear_value] is the new value.

signals.name: ThemeChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the [constant NOTIFICATION_THEME_CHANGED] notification is sent.

signals.name: TitleChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when window title bar text is changed.

signals.name: TitlebarChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when window title bar decorations are changed, e.g. macOS window enter/exit full screen mode, or extend-to-title flag is changed.

signals.name: VisibilityChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when [Window] is made visible or disappears.

signals.name: WindowInput
signals.summary: Emitted when the [Window] is currently focused and receives any input, passing the received event as an argument. The event's position, if present, is in the embedder's coordinate system.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_EXPAND
values.summary: The content's aspect will be preserved. If the target size has different aspect from the base one, the content will stay in the top-left corner and add an extra visible area in the stretched space.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_IGNORE
values.summary: The aspect will be ignored. Scaling will simply stretch the content to fit the target size.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP
values.summary: The content's aspect will be preserved. If the target size has different aspect from the base one, the image will be centered and black bars will appear on left and right sides.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP_HEIGHT
values.summary: The content can be expanded horizontally. Scaling vertically will result in keeping the height ratio and then black bars on top and bottom sides.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP_WIDTH
values.summary: The content can be expanded vertically. Scaling horizontally will result in keeping the width ratio and then black bars on left and right sides.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_CANVAS_ITEMS
values.summary: The content will be rendered at the target size. This is more performance-expensive than [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_VIEWPORT], but provides better results.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: The content will not be scaled to match the [Window]'s size ([member content_scale_size] is ignored).

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_VIEWPORT
values.summary: The content will be rendered at the base size and then scaled to the target size. More performant than [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_CANVAS_ITEMS], but results in pixelated image.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_STRETCH_FRACTIONAL
values.summary: The content will be stretched according to a fractional factor. This fills all the space available in the window, but allows "pixel wobble" to occur due to uneven pixel scaling.

values.name: CONTENT_SCALE_STRETCH_INTEGER
values.summary: The content will be stretched only according to an integer factor, preserving sharp pixels. This may leave a black background visible on the window's edges depending on the window size.

values.name: FLAG_ALWAYS_ON_TOP
values.summary: The window is floating on top of all other windows. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. Set with [member always_on_top]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

values.name: FLAG_BORDERLESS
values.summary: The window do not have native title bar and other decorations. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. Set with [member borderless]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

values.name: FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
values.summary: Windows is excluded from screenshots taken by [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image], [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image_rect], and [method DisplayServer.screen_get_pixel]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on macOS and Windows (10, 20H1). [b]Note:[/b] Setting this flag will prevent standard screenshot methods from capturing a window image, but does [b]NOT[/b] guarantee that other apps won't be able to capture an image. It should not be used as a DRM or security measure.

values.name: FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
values.summary: Window content is expanded to the full size of the window. Unlike borderless window, the frame is left intact and can be used to resize the window, title bar is transparent, but have minimize/maximize/close buttons. Set with [member extend_to_title]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

values.name: FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Max value of the [enum Flags].

values.name: FLAG_MAXIMIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: Window maximize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: FLAG_MINIMIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: Window minimize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: FLAG_MOUSE_PASSTHROUGH
values.summary: All mouse events are passed to the underlying window of the same application. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows.

values.name: FLAG_NO_FOCUS
values.summary: The window can't be focused. No-focus window will ignore all input, except mouse clicks. Set with [member unfocusable]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

values.name: FLAG_POPUP
values.summary: Window is part of menu or [OptionButton] dropdown. This flag can't be changed when the window is visible. An active popup window will exclusively receive all input, without stealing focus from its parent. Popup windows are automatically closed when uses click outside it, or when an application is switched. Popup window must have transient parent set (see [member transient]). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded [Popup] windows.

values.name: FLAG_POPUP_WM_HINT
values.summary: Signals the window manager that this window is supposed to be an implementation-defined "popup" (usually a floating, borderless, untileable and immovable child window). [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (Wayland).

values.name: FLAG_RESIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: The window can't be resized by dragging its resize grip. It's still possible to resize the window using [member size]. This flag is ignored for full screen windows. Set with [member unresizable]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

values.name: FLAG_SHARP_CORNERS
values.summary: Window style is overridden, forcing sharp corners. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Windows (11).

values.name: FLAG_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: The window background can be transparent. Set with [member transparent]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect if either [member ProjectSettings.display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed], or the window's [member Viewport.transparent_bg] is set to [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_APPLICATION_LOCALE
values.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the current locale.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_INHERITED
values.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the parent window layout direction.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_LTR
values.summary: Left-to-right layout direction.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum LayoutDirection] enum.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_RTL
values.summary: Right-to-left layout direction.

values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_SYSTEM_LOCALE
values.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the system locale.

values.name: MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN
values.summary: A single window full screen mode. This mode has less overhead, but only one window can be open on a given screen at a time (opening a child window or application switching will trigger a full screen transition). Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no border or decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]Note:[/b] This mode might not work with screen recording software. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On Windows:[/b] Depending on video driver, full screen transition might cause screens to go black for a moment. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. Exclusive full screen mode prevents Dock and Menu from showing up when the mouse pointer is hovering the edge of the screen. [b]On Linux (X11):[/b] Exclusive full screen mode bypasses compositor. [b]On Linux (Wayland):[/b] Equivalent to [constant MODE_FULLSCREEN]. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

values.name: MODE_FULLSCREEN
values.summary: Full screen mode with full multi-window support. Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

values.name: MODE_MAXIMIZED
values.summary: Maximized window mode, i.e. [Window] will occupy whole screen area except task bar and still display its borders. Normally happens when the maximize button is pressed.

values.name: MODE_MINIMIZED
values.summary: Minimized window mode, i.e. [Window] is not visible and available on window manager's window list. Normally happens when the minimize button is pressed.

values.name: MODE_WINDOWED
values.summary: Windowed mode, i.e. [Window] doesn't occupy the whole screen (unless set to the size of the screen).

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_ABSOLUTE
values.summary: Initial window position is determined by [member position].

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_MAIN_WINDOW_SCREEN
values.summary: Initial window position is the center of the main window screen.

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_OTHER_SCREEN
values.summary: Initial window position is the center of [member current_screen] screen.

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_PRIMARY_SCREEN
values.summary: Initial window position is the center of the primary screen.

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_SCREEN_WITH_KEYBOARD_FOCUS
values.summary: Initial window position is the center of the screen containing the window with the keyboard focus.

values.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_SCREEN_WITH_MOUSE_FOCUS
values.summary: Initial window position is the center of the screen containing the mouse pointer.

constants.name: NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
constants.summary: Emitted when [Window]'s visibility changes, right before [signal visibility_changed].

constants.name: NOTIFICATION_THEME_CHANGED
constants.summary: Sent when the node needs to refresh its theme items. This happens in one of the following cases: - The [member theme] property is changed on this node or any of its ancestors. - The [member theme_type_variation] property is changed on this node. - The node enters the scene tree. [b]Note:[/b] As an optimization, this notification won't be sent from changes that occur while this node is outside of the scene tree. Instead, all of the theme item updates can be applied at once when the node enters the scene tree.

constants.name: MODE_WINDOWED
constants.summary: Windowed mode, i.e. [Window] doesn't occupy the whole screen (unless set to the size of the screen).

constants.name: MODE_MINIMIZED
constants.summary: Minimized window mode, i.e. [Window] is not visible and available on window manager's window list. Normally happens when the minimize button is pressed.

constants.name: MODE_MAXIMIZED
constants.summary: Maximized window mode, i.e. [Window] will occupy whole screen area except task bar and still display its borders. Normally happens when the maximize button is pressed.

constants.name: MODE_FULLSCREEN
constants.summary: Full screen mode with full multi-window support. Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

constants.name: MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN
constants.summary: A single window full screen mode. This mode has less overhead, but only one window can be open on a given screen at a time (opening a child window or application switching will trigger a full screen transition). Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no border or decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]Note:[/b] This mode might not work with screen recording software. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On Windows:[/b] Depending on video driver, full screen transition might cause screens to go black for a moment. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. Exclusive full screen mode prevents Dock and Menu from showing up when the mouse pointer is hovering the edge of the screen. [b]On Linux (X11):[/b] Exclusive full screen mode bypasses compositor. [b]On Linux (Wayland):[/b] Equivalent to [constant MODE_FULLSCREEN]. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

constants.name: FLAG_RESIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: The window can't be resized by dragging its resize grip. It's still possible to resize the window using [member size]. This flag is ignored for full screen windows. Set with [member unresizable]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

constants.name: FLAG_BORDERLESS
constants.summary: The window do not have native title bar and other decorations. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. Set with [member borderless]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

constants.name: FLAG_ALWAYS_ON_TOP
constants.summary: The window is floating on top of all other windows. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. Set with [member always_on_top]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

constants.name: FLAG_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: The window background can be transparent. Set with [member transparent]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect if either [member ProjectSettings.display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed], or the window's [member Viewport.transparent_bg] is set to [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

constants.name: FLAG_NO_FOCUS
constants.summary: The window can't be focused. No-focus window will ignore all input, except mouse clicks. Set with [member unfocusable]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows, and embedded windows.

constants.name: FLAG_POPUP
constants.summary: Window is part of menu or [OptionButton] dropdown. This flag can't be changed when the window is visible. An active popup window will exclusively receive all input, without stealing focus from its parent. Popup windows are automatically closed when uses click outside it, or when an application is switched. Popup window must have transient parent set (see [member transient]). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows, and embedded [Popup] windows.

constants.name: FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
constants.summary: Window content is expanded to the full size of the window. Unlike borderless window, the frame is left intact and can be used to resize the window, title bar is transparent, but have minimize/maximize/close buttons. Set with [member extend_to_title]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

constants.name: FLAG_MOUSE_PASSTHROUGH
constants.summary: All mouse events are passed to the underlying window of the same application. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, Windows.

constants.name: FLAG_SHARP_CORNERS
constants.summary: Window style is overridden, forcing sharp corners. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Windows (11).

constants.name: FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
constants.summary: Windows is excluded from screenshots taken by [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image], [method DisplayServer.screen_get_image_rect], and [method DisplayServer.screen_get_pixel]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on macOS and Windows (10, 20H1). [b]Note:[/b] Setting this flag will prevent standard screenshot methods from capturing a window image, but does [b]NOT[/b] guarantee that other apps won't be able to capture an image. It should not be used as a DRM or security measure.

constants.name: FLAG_POPUP_WM_HINT
constants.summary: Signals the window manager that this window is supposed to be an implementation-defined "popup" (usually a floating, borderless, untileable and immovable child window). [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (Wayland).

constants.name: FLAG_MINIMIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Window minimize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: FLAG_MAXIMIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Window maximize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect in embedded windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Max value of the [enum Flags].

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: The content will not be scaled to match the [Window]'s size ([member content_scale_size] is ignored).

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_CANVAS_ITEMS
constants.summary: The content will be rendered at the target size. This is more performance-expensive than [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_VIEWPORT], but provides better results.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_VIEWPORT
constants.summary: The content will be rendered at the base size and then scaled to the target size. More performant than [constant CONTENT_SCALE_MODE_CANVAS_ITEMS], but results in pixelated image.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_IGNORE
constants.summary: The aspect will be ignored. Scaling will simply stretch the content to fit the target size.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP
constants.summary: The content's aspect will be preserved. If the target size has different aspect from the base one, the image will be centered and black bars will appear on left and right sides.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP_WIDTH
constants.summary: The content can be expanded vertically. Scaling horizontally will result in keeping the width ratio and then black bars on left and right sides.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_KEEP_HEIGHT
constants.summary: The content can be expanded horizontally. Scaling vertically will result in keeping the height ratio and then black bars on top and bottom sides.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_ASPECT_EXPAND
constants.summary: The content's aspect will be preserved. If the target size has different aspect from the base one, the content will stay in the top-left corner and add an extra visible area in the stretched space.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_STRETCH_FRACTIONAL
constants.summary: The content will be stretched according to a fractional factor. This fills all the space available in the window, but allows "pixel wobble" to occur due to uneven pixel scaling.

constants.name: CONTENT_SCALE_STRETCH_INTEGER
constants.summary: The content will be stretched only according to an integer factor, preserving sharp pixels. This may leave a black background visible on the window's edges depending on the window size.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_INHERITED
constants.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the parent window layout direction.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_APPLICATION_LOCALE
constants.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the current locale.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_LTR
constants.summary: Left-to-right layout direction.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_RTL
constants.summary: Right-to-left layout direction.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_SYSTEM_LOCALE
constants.summary: Automatic layout direction, determined from the system locale.

constants.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum LayoutDirection] enum.

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_ABSOLUTE
constants.summary: Initial window position is determined by [member position].

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_PRIMARY_SCREEN
constants.summary: Initial window position is the center of the primary screen.

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_MAIN_WINDOW_SCREEN
constants.summary: Initial window position is the center of the main window screen.

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_OTHER_SCREEN
constants.summary: Initial window position is the center of [member current_screen] screen.

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_SCREEN_WITH_MOUSE_FOCUS
constants.summary: Initial window position is the center of the screen containing the mouse pointer.

constants.name: WINDOW_INITIAL_POSITION_CENTER_SCREEN_WITH_KEYBOARD_FOCUS
constants.summary: Initial window position is the center of the screen containing the window with the keyboard focus.

virtualMethods.name: _GetContentsMinimumSize
virtualMethods.summary: Virtual method to be implemented by the user. Overrides the value returned by [method get_contents_minimum_size].
